Title: Successful user login with valid credentials
Preconditions:
- FirebaseAuth is operational.
- User account exists with valid email/password (e.g., `user@example.com`, `password123`).
Test Steps:
1. User initiates `log_in()` via the application.
2. System calls `checkIfLogin()` to verify existing session.
3. System detects `user == null` and invokes `loggingIn(email, password)`.
4. System forwards credentials to `FirebaseAuth.signInWithEmailAndPassword()`.
5. FirebaseAuth validates credentials and returns a non-null user object.
6. System updates local session with `mAuth.getCurrentUser()`.
Expected Result:
- User is logged in successfully.
- Application displays a confirmation message (e.g., "Login successful").
- Session persists for subsequent operations.

Title: Failed login with invalid credentials
Preconditions:
- FirebaseAuth is operational.
- User account does not exist or credentials are incorrect.
Test Steps:
1. User initiates `log_in()` with invalid credentials.
2. System calls `checkIfLogin()` and detects `user == null`.
3. System invokes `loggingIn(email, password)`.
4. FirebaseAuth rejects credentials (`signInWithEmailAndPassword` returns `null`).
5. System does not update local session.
Expected Result:
- Application displays an error message (e.g., "Invalid email or password").
- User remains logged out.
- No session is created.

Title: Successful user registration and login
Preconditions:
- FirebaseAuth is operational.
- No existing account for the provided email.
Test Steps:
1. User initiates `log_in()` with a non-existent email.
2. System detects `user == null` and invokes `registerAndLogin(email, password)`.
3. System calls `FirebaseAuth.createUserWithEmailAndPassword()`.
4. FirebaseAuth creates the account and returns a non-null user object.
5. System updates local session with `mAuth.getCurrentUser()`.
Expected Result:
- User account is created successfully.
- Application displays a confirmation message (e.g., "Account created and logged in").
- Session persists for subsequent operations.

Title: Failed registration due to invalid email format
Test Steps:
1. User attempts to register with invalid email format.
2. System validates input before calling `createUserWithEmailAndPassword()`.
3. FirebaseAuth rejects the request due to invalid email format.
Expected Result:
- Application displays an error message (e.g., "Please enter a valid email address").
- No account is created.
- User remains logged out.

Title: Already logged-in user attempts to log in again
Preconditions: - User is already logged in (session exists).
Test Steps:
1. User initiates `log_in()`.
2. System calls `checkIfLogin()` and detects an existing session (`user != null`).
3. System skips `loggingIn()` or `registerAndLogin()`.
Expected Result:
- Application detects existing session and displays a message (e.g., "Already logged in").
- No redundant login attempt is made.
- Session remains active.

Title: Retrieve and display existing signal reports
Preconditions: - Database contains at least one `Segnalazione` record.
Test Data: - Expected payload: `[{id: 1, title: "Track issue", description: "Obstacle on track", date: "2023-10-01T10:00:00"}]`
Test Steps:
1. Application calls `getSegnalazioni()`.
2. `Sistema gestione segnalazioni` forwards request to `Sistema gestione dati`.
3. `Sistema gestione dati` queries database with `SELECT Segnalazioni(*)`.
4. Database returns payload with signal reports.
5. Payload is forwarded back to the application.
Expected Result:
- Application displays a list of signal reports with details (title, description, date).
- No `Null` is displayed.

Title: Handle empty signal report list
Test Steps:
1. Application calls `getSegnalazioni()`.
2. Database returns an empty payload.
3. System forwards `Null` to the application.
Expected Result:
- Application displays a message (e.g., "No signals reported yet").
- No list is shown.

Title: Add a new signal report
Preconditions:
- User is logged in.
- `GestoreSegnalazioni` is operational.
Test Data: - New signal: `{id: 2, title: "Signal failure", description: "Red light malfunction", date: "2023-10-02T11:30:00", utente: "user@example.com"}`
Test Steps:
1. User calls `addSegnalazione(Segnalazione)` with valid data.
2. `GestoreSegnalazioni` validates input (non-empty title/description).
3. System persists the signal to the database.
Expected Result:
- Signal is added successfully.
- `getSegnalazioni()` now includes the new report.
- Application displays a confirmation (e.g., "Signal reported successfully").

Title: Add signal report with invalid data
Test Data: - Invalid signal: `{id: 0, title: "", description: "", date: "invalid-date", utente: ""}`
Test Steps:
1. User calls `addSegnalazione(Segnalazione)` with empty/invalid fields.
2. `GestoreSegnalazioni` detects invalid data (e.g., empty title).
Expected Result:
- Application displays an error (e.g., "Title and description are required").
- Signal is not added to the database.

Title: Modify an existing signal report
Preconditions:
- Database contains a signal with `id: 1`.
- User is authorized to modify signals.
Test Data: - Updated signal: `{id: 1, title: "Updated title", description: "Updated description", date: "2023-10-02T12:00:00"}`
Test Steps:
1. User calls `modifySegnalazione(Segnalazione, id)` with valid data.
2. `GestoreSegnalazioni` validates the update.
3. System updates the record in the database.
Expected Result:
- Signal is updated successfully.
- `getSegnalazioni()` reflects the changes.
- Application displays a confirmation (e.g., "Signal updated successfully").

Title: Remove a signal report
Preconditions:
- Database contains a signal with `id: 1`.
- User is authorized to delete signals.
Test Steps:
1. User calls `removeSegnalazione(id)` with `id: 1`.
2. `GestoreSegnalazioni` validates the ID exists.
3. System deletes the record from the database.
Expected Result:
- Signal is removed successfully.
- `getSegnalazioni()` no longer includes the deleted report.
- Application displays a confirmation (e.g., "Signal deleted successfully").

Title: Remove non-existent signal report
Test Steps:
1. User calls `removeSegnalazione(id)` with `id: 999`.
2. `GestoreSegnalazioni` detects the ID does not exist.
Expected Result:
- Application displays an error (e.g., "Signal not found").
- No deletion occurs.

Title: Retrieve and display calendar data
Test Data: - Expected payload: `Calendario` with `Giorno[7]` (e.g., days of the week with train schedules).
Test Steps:
1. Application calls `getCalendario()`.
2. `Sistema gestione calendario` forwards the request to `Sistema gestione dati`.
3. Database returns the `Calendario` payload.
4. Payload is displayed in the application.
Expected Result:
- Application shows the calendar with all 7 days.
- No `Null` is displayed.

Title: Handle empty calendar data
Test Steps:
1. Application calls `getCalendario()`.
2. Database returns `Null`.
3. Application displays `Null`.
Expected Result:
- Application shows a message (e.g., "No calendar data available").
- No calendar is rendered.

Title: Update calendar data
Preconditions:
- User is authorized to update the calendar.
- New `Calendario` data is valid.
Test Steps:
1. Application calls `setCalendario(cal)` with new data.
2. `Sistema gestione calendario` forwards the update to `Sistema gestione dati`.
3. Database processes the update and returns a success status code.
4. Outcome is displayed in the application.
Expected Result:
- Calendar is updated successfully in the database.
- `getCalendario()` reflects the changes.
- Application displays a confirmation (e.g., "Calendar updated successfully").

Title: Update calendar with invalid data
Test Data: - Invalid calendar: `Calendario` with missing or malformed `Giorno` entries.
Test Steps:
1. Application calls `setCalendario(cal)` with invalid data.
2. `Sistema gestione calendario` detects invalid structure (e.g., fewer than 7 days).
Expected Result:
- Application displays an error (e.g., "Invalid calendar data").
- No update occurs in the database.
